title: Yamarus
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}}

{{Quote|Many lives have been sacrificed already for our efforts…but many more will be lost if we do not succeed.|Yamarus|audio=Yamarus1-XWA.flac|<ref name="xwa" />}}
'''Yamarus''' was an [[Admiral/Legends|admiral]] in the [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|fleet]] of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]. Commanding the [[MC80 Liberty Type Heavy Star Cruiser/Legends|MC80 Star Cruiser]] [[Liberty/Legends|''Liberty'']] and its [[Liberty Task Force|task force]], Yamarus served in a variety of campaigns against both the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] forces in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]]. Following the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]], he worked to destroy [[Director/Legends|Director]] [[Lenzer]]'s [[TIE Experimental Project]], which involved the development of [[Droid/Legends|droid]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] and enhanced [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]]. With the help of his pilots, Yamarus was able to eradicate the project entirely. Subsequently, he received multiple transmissions from turncoat Imperial officer [[Zaletta]], which allowed the admiral to strike several critical blows against the Empire.

Following [[Battle of Eidoloni|Zaletta's defection]], the pair worked together to determine the nature of a top-secret Imperial construction project. Working with [[Koth Melan]] and [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], Yamarus's pilots were instrumental in acquiring and protecting data that identified the new construction as the [[Death Star II/Legends|Death Star II]]. Realizing that such a [[Superweapon/Legends|weapon]] could spell doom for the Alliance, Yamarus worked to gain a new ally in the form of [[Dunari]], an entrepreneur and former [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]]. Though Dunari and Yamarus were constantly harassed by the [[Hurrim]] mercenary group, they were able to establish a strong coalition together. With a strong infrastructure in place, Yamarus began to work towards building up the [[Fleet/Legends|fleet's]] strength for the coming attack on the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]]. Yamarus's soldiers procured the [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] [[Tydirium/Legends|''Tydirium'']], a [[Starship/Legends|ship]] that had clearance to land on the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]], home to the [[Planetary shield/Legends|shield generator]] that protected the Death Star [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]]. Yamarus's efforts proved to be instrumental in the defeat of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]].

==Biography==
===TIE Experimental Project===
{{Quote|This offers us an extraordinary opportunity to strike a major blow against the Empire…an offer I will not refuse.|Yamarus|audio=Yamarus2-XWA.flac|<ref name="xwa" />}}
Yamarus served the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] as an [[Admiral/Legends|admiral]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] and was given command of an [[MC80 Liberty Type Heavy Star Cruiser/Legends|MC80 Star Cruiser]], the [[Liberty/Legends|''Liberty'']], as well as its accompanying [[Liberty Task Force|task force]]. Following the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]]<ref name="xwa" /> in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> the ''Liberty'' was sent to the [[Vilonis sector/Legends|Vilonis sector]], sans task force, to investigate reports of attacks on neutral [[Freighter/Legends|freighters]]. Having lost some support staff recently, the ''Liberty'' received a transfer of [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] and officers from the [[MC80A Home One Type Heavy Star Cruiser/Legends|MC80 Star Cruiser]] [[Defiance/Legends|''Defiance'']]. One of the pilots, [[Ace Azzameen/Legends|Ace Azzameen]], investigated a distress signal during the transfer with his wingman. After a skirmish in the [[Belat system/Legends|Belat system]], Azzameen reported that he had helped save a civilian convoy from destruction at the hands of unusually modified [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE Fighters]]. Pleased that he had a new lead in his investigation, Yamarus had his technicians review Azzameen's flight recorder data, while welcoming the new pilot to the ''Liberty''.<ref name="xwa" />

Hoping to inspect some of the mysterious craft at closer range, Yamarus had a set of [[Droid/Legends|droid]]-piloted [[MB-C1 medium transport|Mobquet medium transports]] from [[Sulin]] Group travel around the [[Sector/Legends|sector]], utilizing fake transponders and dodging [[Imperial Office of Customs/Legends|customs checkpoints]]. The admiral intended for the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperials]] to take the bait and attempt to destroy the convoy with their [[TIE Experimental Project|experimental fighters]]. Hoping to ensnare these attackers, he worked with his new starfighter officer, [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Kupalo]], to devise a trap. Since he hoped to keep a low profile in the sector, Yamarus chose to have [[Z-95 Headhunter/Legends|Z-95 Headhunters]] deployed, although they would be equipped with [[Ion missile/Legends|ion pulse warheads]] to help disable the Imperial craft.<ref name="xwa" />

The operation was a success, with two of the new fighter variants brought in, as well as one of the [[Beta-class ETR-3 escort transport|''Beta''-class ETR-3 escort transports]] that accompanied the attackers. Before Yamarus's staff had an opportunity to question the crew of the [[Transport/Legends|transport]], however, they learned that its [[Life support/Legends|life-support system]] had "malfunctioned" upon the disabling of the [[Starship/Legends|ship]]. What his technicians did learn was that the transports were equipped with elaborate communication devices, used to control the unmanned fighters. Yamarus concluded that the new TIEs were a part of an experimental program, although he had no idea as to who the arbiter could be.<ref name="xwa" />

[[File:TIEExperimentalProjectMission04-XWA.png|thumb|left|220px|The ''Liberty'' falls under starfighter attack.]]
[[Alliance Intelligence/Legends|Alliance Intelligence]] was able to provide a lead, as one of their agents had established that the [[Bretie Facility]] in the [[Kurdin system]] was being used to develop the experimental fighters. With orders to destroy the facility once they had extracted any pertinent information, [[Storm Unit (team)|Storm Unit]] was sent by Yamarus to sweep Bretie for valuable data. Before the data could be looked into, however, the ''Liberty'' was beset by multiple squadrons of the experimental TIE craft, which had tracked and followed Storm Unit. Yamarus and Kupalo rushed to scramble all fighters, hoping to buy time for the ''Liberty'' to escape. Thanks to the efforts of the pilots, the attackers were completely driven off.<ref name="xwa" />

With the data decrypted, the Alliance confirmed that the experimental project was exploring the use of droid-piloted craft. The project, headed by [[Director/Legends|Director]] [[Lenzer]], was top-secret—no files on it or its director were found by Intelligence agents at all. Faced with the threat of an inexhaustible supply of totally loyal pilots in the [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor's]] control, Yamarus became even more determined to stop the project at its source. Dispatching reconnaissance flights, he located [[Obsidian (XQ1 Platform)|Obsidian]], Lenzer's center of operations. Wasting no time, Yamarus had his fighters demolish the [[Space station/Legends|station]] and every last experimental TIE. A master control ship, the ''[[Sardis]]'', was also destroyed, in the hopes that the Empire would be dissuaded from attempting another similar project in the future.<ref name="xwa" />

===Zaletta's defection===
Following the experimental TIE incident, the ''Liberty'' was re-teamed with its task force. While monitoring the Imperial communications network, Yamarus's men located a transmission sent with an outmoded Alliance code. Though the transmission lasted only several seconds, the technicians determined that it came from deep within [[Imperial Space|Imperial space]], where the Alliance had no presence whatsoever. The transmission contained coordinates for a [[Slavery/Legends|slave]] convoy transfer and insisted that the Alliance would be interested in these particular slaves. Yamarus sent out fighters to determine the accuracy of the claim and had assault teams on standby just in case it was true. Sure enough, [[Tango Group]] returned with the slaves: [[Bothan/Legends|Bothans]] with Alliance sympathies that had been arrested simply due to their beliefs. [[Alliance High Command/Legends|Alliance High Command]] grew interested in Yamarus's source and requested that he look further into it.<ref name="xwa" />

Shortly thereafter, Yamarus received another anonymous transmission, this time claiming that an Imperial [[Kuat Facility|research facility]] in the [[Kuat system/Legends|Kuat system]] was vulnerable to Alliance attack. Cautious, the admiral again sent out reconnaissance flights to determine the validity of the claim, before dispatching an attack force. Fearing that it might be a trap, Yamarus ordered his pilots to knock out the facility's communications array first, before Imperial reinforcements could be called in. When it was determined that the facility was used for testing experimental [[Reactor/Legends|reactors]], High Command approved of its destruction.<ref name="xwa" />

After the [[Kuat/Legends|Kuat]] attack, the admiral learned that although the informant was using Alliance codes, he was using Imperial equipment to broadcast the messages. Hoping to pinpoint the location of their curious informant, Yamarus sent his fighters and the [[CR92a Assassin-class corvette|''Assassin''-class corvette]] ''[[Python]]'' to the relay station [[Comm Center]], hoping to inspect the apparatus and extract any pertinent information. During the operation, however, the ''Python'' received a transmission from the informant, who claimed to be an Imperial officer seeking asylum with the Alliance. Yamarus dutifully forwarded the request to High Command.<ref name="xwa" />

Command approved the request, and Yamarus sent Commander Kupalo aboard the [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate]] [[Redemption/Legends|''Redemption'']] to discuss the terms of defection. With security tight due to the defector's high-ranking position in the Empire, the admiral sent his fighter squadrons to [[Battle of Eidoloni|attack]] platform [[DX-11a]] in the [[Eidoloni system/Legends|Eidoloni system]], where the defector was stationed. Yamarus hoped that the attack would force an evacuation of the base, allowing his contact to slip away without arousing Imperial suspicion. The operation went smoothly as the defector, who identified himself as [[Zaletta]], escaped in [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] ''[[AA-23]]''.<ref name="xwa" />

However, when he boarded the ''Redemption'', the [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial II''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Subjugator (Imperial II-class)|''Subjugator'']] and the [[Immobilizer 418 Interdictor-class Heavy Cruiser|Immobilizer 418 cruiser]] ''[[Restrainer]]'' entered the [[Star system/Legends|system]], preventing the Alliance [[Frigate/Legends|frigate]] from leaving the battle. Enraged, Kupalo had Zaletta arrested, and then contacted Yamarus with a distress call. Taking the ''Liberty'' to the intended rendezvous, the admiral had his ship engage the ''Restrainer'', allowing the ''Redemption'' to depart. Yamarus soon followed suit, and although Zaletta insisted that he had not set up an Imperial ambush, he was kept in the ''Liberty''{{'s}} holding cells.<ref name="xwa" />

The situation was complicated further when an explosion rocked one the ''Liberty''{{'s}} [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] bays. Commander Kupalo had hijacked ''AA-23'' and killed several Alliance technicians and pilots in the process, before departing the system. Rushing to brief his pilots himself, Yamarus sent out his craft to reel in the renegade. Although Kupalo nearly reached the ''Imperial I''-class Star Destroyer [[Imperator (Imperial-class)|''Imperator'']], he was forced to power down his engines by Ace Azzameen, and then retrieved by Storm Unit. Kupalo had been brainwashed before his rescue by the Alliance several months before, and was now working as an Imperial [[Spy/Legends|agent]]. Although Yamarus was disheartened by the fact that Kupalo would likely never recover, he could now clear Zaletta's name and have his technicians extract vital information from ''AA-23''.<ref name="xwa" /> The admiral praised pilots Azzameen and Garn for their part in Kupalo's capture.<ref name="XWA:OSG">[[Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]]</ref>

===Intelligence and counter-intelligence===
{{Quote|We must do everything in our power to destroy this battle station before it becomes fully operational.|Yamarus|audio=Yamarus3-XWA.flac|<ref name="xwa" />}}
Zaletta, hoping to prove his value to the Alliance, claimed that the Imperials had been moving large convoys through the Eidoloni system, although the logs he had brought with him on ''AA-23'' did not specify the contents of the ships, which was unusual. Alliance Intelligence confirmed Zaletta's claims, and they also discovered that the convoys were accompanied by a large escort, which tended to mean that the goods being shipped were of the utmost importance. The admiral sent out two [[RZ-1 A-wing interceptor/Legends|A-wings]] from [[Blue Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Blue Squadron]] to determine just what the Empire deemed so precious. Blue Squadron discovered that a huge amount of raw material was being shipped by [[Xizor Transport Systems/Legends|Xizor Transport Systems]], one of the biggest conglomerates in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], and one with Imperial ties. Yamarus speculated that the Empire might have been trying to expand their [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyards]] or establish a new base of operations in the sector.<ref name="xwa" />

The Alliance was subsequently contacted by [[Koth Melan]], a [[Bothan Spynet/Legends|Bothan spy]] who had gained intelligence of a fertilizer freighter headed for [[Bothawui/Legends|Bothawui]] that supposedly contained the technical readouts of a new Imperial construction project. Yamarus found it curious that the information had come from the notorious criminal organization [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]], but decided to send aid anyway. The freighter, ''[[Suprosa]]'', was to be [[Raid on the Suprosa|disabled]] and boarded by Commander [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], the [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] [[Dash Rendar/Legends|Dash Rendar]], and a squadron of Bothan [[BTL-S3 Y-wing starfighter/Legends|BTL-S3 Y-wing starfighter]] pilots. The Bothans, however, had one vacancy, which prompted Yamarus to offer the services of Ace Azzameen due to the young pilot's exemplary record.<ref name="xwa" />

[[File:LibertyAndStarfighters-XWA-DAT15210-2002.png|thumb|right|280px|Yamarus's ship, the ''Liberty'', with fighter escort]]
While Skywalker and his team retrieved the plans, Yamarus took the ''Liberty'' to [[Kothlis/Legends|Kothlis]] to await delivery of the ''Suprosa''{{'s}} data. Though they ran into Imperial entanglements on the way, Skywalker returned with the plans and took them to the Rebel base on Kothlis for decryption. The Empire, however, wasted no time whatsoever in attempting to retrieve the stolen plans. [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], [[Supreme Commander (Galactic Empire)/Legends|Supreme Commander]] of the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]], led the [[Battle of Kothlis|attack]] on Kothlis. As his [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|Star Dreadnought]], the [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']], bore down on the Bothan colony world, the Rebel safe house was attacked by [[Barabel/Legends|Barabel]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]], with Melan dying in the ensuing firefight.<ref name="sote">[[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (novel)|''Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire'' novel]]</ref> One Bothan, [[Borsk Fey'lya]], had managed to escape with the [[Computer/Legends|computer]] core, and was en route to the Alliance platform [[Kothlis II]]. Yamarus hoped to be able to send the plans to the Alliance [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], Admiral [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar's]] MC80 Star Cruiser [[Independence/Legends|''Independence'']], where they would be considerably safer.<ref name="xwa" />

While the platform [[Sentinel (Golan defense platform)|Sentinel]] attempted to hold off the ''Executor'' and the ''Imperial II''-class Star Destroyer [[Avenger/Legends|''Avenger'']], the ''Liberty'' oversaw the evacuation of Kothlis II. Fey'lya was delayed, but he was able to deliver the data safely to the CR90 [[Razor (CR90 corvette)|''Razor'']]. While the ''Razor'' escaped, Sentinel fell prey to the [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolasers]] of the ''Executor'', prompting Vader to turn his attention to Kothlis II. The ''Liberty'', sustaining heavy fire from the attacking ships, managed to escape safely. Yamarus was now convinced that the data was of the utmost value, if the ''Executor'' was being sent after it.<ref name="xwa" />

Yamarus's suspicions proved to be correct, as the Imperials did not relent in their pursuit of the plans. [[Alpha-class Xg-1 Star Wing/Legends|''Alpha''-class Xg-1 Star Wings]] disabled the ''Razor'' and were waiting for a ship to arrive and board it. Ackbar decided to take the ''Independence'' to the ''Razor'' in the hopes of offering aid, but couldn't make it in time, prompting Yamarus to dispatch fighters. He hoped that his pilots would be able to drive off the gunboats and safeguard the ''Razor'' until Ackbar arrived. Though the ''Avenger'' and the ''Executor'' arrived on the scene, one of the ''Razor''{{'s}} crew members was able to escape with the data and make it to the ''Independence'' with the aid of the [[Dreadnaught-class heavy cruiser/Legends|''Dreadnaught''-class heavy cruiser]] [[Mercury (Dreadnaught-class)|''Mercury'']]. Ackbar sent Yamarus his thanks for the fighter aid, but, in a shocking revelation, the Alliance discovered the Imperial construction project was in fact a new [[Death Star II/Legends|Death Star]]. Hoping to find out more about the new [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]], High Command ordered the [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] to disperse.<ref name="xwa" />

===Desperate measures===
{{Quote|The time to strike is now.|Yamarus|audio=Yamarus4-XWA.flac|<ref name="xwa" />}}
High Command hoped that the [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Alliance Fleet]] would be able to gain enough momentum and power to destroy the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]] before it was completed, but the Alliance suffered a major setback at the [[Battle of Gal Milnor]]. Not only did the Alliance lose their main supply center on the planet, but also the bulk of their freighter fleet, which was in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]]. As High Command despaired, [[General/Legends|General]] [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] presented a solution: that the Alliance band together with a coalition of [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]], headed by [[Dunari]]. The smugglers would be able to procure the needed supplies and provide their own ships as freighters. Though dubious as to Dunari's legitimacy, Yamarus sent a delegate to meet with the entrepreneur in a secluded area of [[Realspace/Legends|space]].<ref name="xwa" />

Though [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] and Imperials happened upon the rendezvous, Dunari agreed to aid the Alliance in exchange for escort during raids against Imperials. Dunari also provided Yamarus with some intelligence: Admiral [[Garreth Holtz]] was in charge of the Imperial presence that had been harrying the Alliance in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. This was due to the fact that he had let the ''Defiance'' task force slip out of his clutches in months prior. Yamarus surmised that Holtz would not make the same mistake twice. As Dunari began supplying the Alliance with freighters, the Alliance sent fighters to aid [[Golov Nakhym]], leader of the [[Hurrim]] mercenary force and member of the coalition. Nakhym was targeting a civilian convoy carrying Imperial goods, and he agreed to split the takings with the Alliance if they were to lend assistance. Yamarus agreed, but only on the condition that the civilians were not harmed.<ref name="xwa" />

During the mission, however, the Hurrim went back on their agreement and began to attack the civilians. Quick thinking on the part of Yamarus's pilots saved the day for the Alliance, but Yamarus feared that some worlds would now look at them as nothing more than a bunch of rogues. Dunari subsequently kicked Nakhym out of his coalition, but Yamarus's suspicion of the "entrepreneur" only deepened. Nevertheless, he sent his fighters to aid in the [[Battle of Dunari's Rest|evacuation]] of [[Dunari's Rest]], Dunari's [[Casino/Legends|casino]] and the center of operations for the coalition. Dunari suspected that Nakhym had been selling secrets to the Empire and resolved to go after him. High Command was being exasperated by reports of the Hurrim attacking civilians in the name of the Alliance, and the Rebel diplomats were hard-pressed to keep up. Yamarus, understanding that the situation needed remedied swiftly, agreed to help Dunari track down and defeat the Hurrim.<ref name="xwa" />

Dunari's anger at Nakhym proved to be well-founded: during the evacuation of Dunari's casino, the Hurrim had attacked the coalition and captured several of the board members in a bid for power in the sector. Several of the board members were able to escape on the shuttle ''[[Merte]]'' and reached [[Camen Spaceport]] before being pursued by Hurrim starfighters. Yamarus hastily sent his fighters to escort the ''Merte'' and its passengers back to the ''Liberty''. When the board members recovered from their injuries at hands of the Hurrim, they divulged the location of [[Hurrim Base]], allowing the admiral to stop the threat once and for all.<ref name="xwa" />

During the [[Battle of Hurrim Base]], Nakhym was captured, and any remaining coalition freighters under Hurrim control were repossessed. Holtz attempted to come to the aid of his allies in the ''Imperial II''-class Star Destroyer [[Corrupter (Imperial-class)|''Corrupter'']], but was in turn destroyed by the combined forces of the Alliance and Dunari, who arrived in the ''Dreadnaught''-class heavy cruiser [[Longshot (Dreadnaught-class)|''Longshot'']] to lend aid. With the Hurrim threat eliminated, Yamarus devoted all his time and resources to gathering intelligence on the second Death Star.<ref name="xwa" />

===Diplomacy, theft, and final preparations===
[[Council]]or Borsk Fey'lya, owing the Alliance a debt of gratitude for rescuing Bothan slaves in months prior, agreed to [[Mission to meet Bothan delegation|meet]] with members of the Alliance on behalf of the Bothan Spynet to discuss Death Star reconnaissance. After Fey'lya arrived in the Dreadnaught [[Tal'cara (Dreadnaught-class)|''Tal'cara'']], his journey to the ''Liberty'' in the shuttle [[Jade (Lambda-class)|''Jade'']] was beset by attacks from an unidentified group of mercenaries. Zaletta's pilots were able to drive them off, but not before capturing one of their [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pods]]. The mercenary inside the pod claimed that he had been hired by the [[Sullustan/Legends|Sullustans]] to attack the Bothans. Yamarus was disturbed by the news, as [[Sullust/Legends|Sullust]] was a member of the Alliance. Apparently, several years prior, the Bothans had gained knowledge of an Imperial reprisal against Sullust but had failed to divulge the information, resulting in the deaths of many Sullustans. Though it looked like revenge, the Sullustans denied that they had any degree of involvement. Despite the attack, Fey'lya and his delegation agreed to send spies to the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]], where they would investigate the Death Star construction site and hurry back to High Command as quickly as possible.<ref name="xwa" />

Thanks to the efforts of the [[Azzameen family]], the mercenary base was discovered to be, ironically, the [[Azzameen Station/Legends|Azzameen Home Base]], which had been converted for their use. [[Emon Azzameen|Emon]] and [[Aeron Azzameen]], as well as their family droid, [[MK-09/Legends|MK-09]], offered to join their brother, Ace, in the Alliance attack on the station. Yamarus believed that the station's data banks would store any information that might connect the mercenaries to the Bothans, but he feared that the occupants might destroy the base if the battle turned against them. Dispatching Storm Unit to join the attack, the admiral hoped that the station could be captured and converted for Alliance use.<ref name="xwa" />

The operation was a success, and Aeron Azzameen began working with the Alliance techs to scour the base's data banks. Yamarus was particularly impressed with the work of Ace Azzameen in the battle and believed that his family should have been proud of him and his actions. Little time was given to celebrate, however, as Yamarus received word that the Bothan spies that had been inspecting the Death Star had been stopped by the ''Imperial II'' Star Destroyer [[Emancipator|''Accuser'']] and the [[Interdictor/Legends|Interdictor]] [[Grappler (Immobilizer 418 cruiser)|''Grappler'']], and the cruisers they were hiding on were awaiting inspection. With the intelligence the Bothans held being so valuable to the war effort, Yamarus dispatched his fighters post-haste to remedy the situation.<ref name="xwa" />

[[File:Liberty2.jpg|thumb|left|280px|The ''Liberty'' as of 4 ABY]]
Though some of the Bothans perished in the ensuing skirmish, Fey'lya appreciated the Alliance's efforts to rescue those who survived. However, Zaletta had contacted the Sullustans for help during the small battle, and they had refused, provoking the Bothan councilor's rage. Nevertheless, the intelligence from the Bothan spies revealed that the second Death Star was protected by an [[Planetary shield/Legends|energy shield]], located on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of Endor. They had also learned that a [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] called [[Tydirium/Legends|''Tydirium'']] had the required clearance codes to land on the planet. Yamarus and Zaletta agreed that procuring the ''Tydirium'' would be key in securing victory for the Alliance in the long run.<ref name="xwa" />

Acting on the intelligence, Yamarus decided to send a disguised freighter to the [[Zhar system]], insert a commando team, and bring the ''Tydirium'' back to the ''Liberty''. To pilot the freighter, he selected Ace Azzameen, who had worked with his family's shipping company before it was shut down by the Empire. With the help of the elite [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]], Yamarus's commandos [[Mission to Zhar|retrieved]] the ''Tydirium'', allowing the final preparations for the attack on Endor to begin. Simultaneously, the Sullustans explained that the Empire had hired mercenaries to attack the Bothans in their name, hoping to destroy Sullust's credibility and tear apart the Alliance from within. With evidence provided in the form of the mercenary leader, who the Sullustans had captured, Yamarus was convinced that they were cleared of any suspected foul play.<ref name="xwa" />

Holding station at Sullust, Yamarus received word that both the ''Defiance'' and the ''Independence'' were being held up by the Imperials. Sending a group of A-wings and his most elite pilots, including [[Olin Garn]] and Ace Azzameen, Yamarus hoped that his colleagues would be able to reach the rendezvous in time, as an assault on the Death Star with just the ''Liberty'' task force would be suicidal. Sure enough, both the ''Defiance'' and the ''Independence'' were able to [[Battle of Sullust (4 ABY)|make it]] to Sullust.<ref name="xwa" /> Shortly thereafter, in early [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]],<ref name="NEC" /> the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|attack]] on the Death Star took place, with the ''Tydirium'' ferrying a group of commandos led by General [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], who succeeded in knocking out the battle station's energy shield. The Alliance Fleet destroyed the Death Star, and both Darth Vader and Emperor [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] perished, leaving the Alliance the victors of one of the largest battles of the Galactic Civil War. However, during the battle, the ''Liberty'' fell prey to the battle station's [[Superlaser/Legends|superlaser]].<ref name="rotj">{{Film|VI}}</ref>

==Personality and traits==
A proud and dedicated man, Admiral Yamarus believed strongly in the cause of the Rebel Alliance. He would risk both his ship and his life on moral issues, defending the helpless and weak at the expense of power. He cared for his pilots and respected them, particularly lavishing praise upon young Ace Azzameen. He would also leap to the defense of his allies, personally taking his ship into the heat of battle to save the ''Redemption'' during Zaletta's defection.<ref name="xwa" />

Yamarus was also a suspicious man, and he did not trust known criminals or characters of dubious repute. It took a great deal for Dunari to convince the admiral of his good intention, and Yamarus would not trust certain organizations at all. He was occasionally exasperated by politicians such as Borsk Fey'lya,<ref name="xwa" /> but was never as vocal about their maneuvering as some of his colleagues.<ref name="htte">''[[Heir to the Empire]]''</ref><ref name="vp">''[[The New Jedi Order: Vector Prime]]''</ref> Even though he was a military man, Yamarus would sometimes have to act as a politician himself, meditating disputes between races and improving the public view of the Alliance. The admiral was exceptionally critical of Imperial policy and was disgusted by projects such as that of the experimental TIEs, which used civilians for target practice.<ref name="xwa" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|We are depending on you to put an immediate and conclusive end to this Director Lenzer's scheme. Good luck.|Jarion Monroe as Admiral Yamarus|audio=Yamarus.flac|<ref name="xwa" />}}
Admiral Yamarus was created for [[Lawrence Holland]]'s [[1999]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance]]'', which was written by [[David Wessman]]. Portrayed by [[Jarion Monroe]], the character is never actually seen in the game—instead, he provides the character with the "Commander Briefing:" a general overview of the upcoming mission and any story context surrounding it. The Yamarus character is the most common provider of the "Commander Briefing" in the game, with the other three characters to do so being MK-09, Admirals [[Nammo]] and Ackbar, as well as [[Antan Azzameen|Antan]], [[Tomaas Azzameen/Legends|Tomaas]], Emon, and Aeron Azzameen.<ref name="xwa" />

While Yamarus's ship, the ''Liberty'', is shown destroyed at the Battle of Endor in ''Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance'', [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']] and its various adaptations, it is not known if he was still commanding it at that point.<ref name="xwa" /><ref name="rotj" />
